title: Quantile Spline Photometric Basis
summary: Encode magnitudes, colors, redshift, and sky coordinates with target-free B-spline basis expansions so simple models can learn smooth nonlinear class boundaries.
feature_strategy: Start from raw alpha, delta, u, g, r, i, z, redshift, spectral_type, galaxy_population, and compact adjacent/broad color indices. For continuous anchors such as ugriz magnitudes, u-g, g-r, r-i, i-z, u-r, g-i, r-z, u-z, redshift, and delta, add quantile-knot cubic B-spline basis columns with constant extrapolation; for alpha add periodic spline basis over 0-360 degrees. Optionally add a small tensor-product block only for high-value pairs such as redshift x u-g, redshift x g-r, redshift x r-i, and g-r x r-i by multiplying low-rank spline basis columns. One-hot encode the two categorical columns. Fit all spline knots without class labels inside each CV fold, and refit on full training covariates for test transformation. Do not use target encodings, auxiliary labels, neighbor features, row id, or intermediate supervised predictors.
baseline_model_panel: Use a simple, reusable balanced panel: a regularized multinomial linear classifier on standardized spline features, a class-weighted random-forest or extra-trees style model, and one class-weighted gradient-boosted tree model such as LightGBM or CatBoost with the competition's balanced-class setting.
model_panel_rationale: The linear model directly tests whether the spline basis makes nonlinear photometric boundaries linearly usable, while the tree models check whether the smoother basis helps or is redundant against native split-based nonlinearities.
validation_strategy: Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold on the multiclass target and report balanced accuracy overall plus per-class recall for GALAXY, STAR, and QSO; fit spline transformers within each training fold only to avoid validation-distribution preprocessing leakage.
materialization_hint: Implement a preprocessing stage that constructs base colors, then applies sklearn-style SplineTransformer blocks per continuous feature group, using sparse output if available. Keep knot counts modest, drop constant or near-duplicate columns, standardize only for linear models, and pass categorical one-hots/native categoricals as appropriate for each baseline model.
expected_signal: If valuable, the regularized linear model should improve noticeably versus raw colors because smooth color-redshift and magnitude regimes become explicit; tree models may show smaller gains but should improve minority-class recall if nonlinear QSO/STAR regions are captured cleanly.
risk: This overlaps weakly with prior quantile-bin and rank/CDF hypotheses because all discretize continuous distributions, but it is a distinct smooth basis-expansion mechanism rather than hard bins or tail summaries. Too many knots or tensor products can overfit or create high-dimensional slow matrices, so the first test should keep the basis compact.

autogluon_feature_transfer.source: legacy feature engineering only; model, fold, calibration, and blending logic intentionally omitted
